description_long: This object is a cylindrical hand grenade labeled 'HMX HI-EXPLOSIVE GRENADE' with a matte dark gray body and a prominent red band near the top. The front features large white text and technical details, while the back displays a '40 M' marking, indicating a 40-meter minimum safe distance. The top is fitted with a robust metal safety lever and a circular pull ring, both rendered in a metallic gray. The base and top have angular, reinforced segments, suggesting durability and secure containment. The surface is smooth with subtle panel lines and bolts, giving it a modern, industrial appearance. The overall design is compact, functional, and clearly intended for manual deployment in tactical scenarios.
description: A compact, cylindrical high-explosive grenade with a matte gray body, red safety band, and metallic safety lever and pull ring. Prominent white labeling and reinforced segments give it a modern, tactical look.
description_view_0: The grenade is shown from the side, highlighting the safety lever, pull ring, and a vertical serial number along the body.
description_view_1: The front view displays the 'HMX HI-EXPLOSIVE GRENADE' label and technical details in large white text, with the safety lever and pull ring clearly visible.
description_view_2: The rear side is mostly plain, showing the smooth gray surface and the red band, with minimal text or features.
description_view_3: The back view features the '40 M MINIMUM SAFE DISTANCE' marking and a diagram, with the safety lever and pull ring visible from the side.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: Detonation is likely initiated by pulling the safety pin and releasing the lever, which would trigger an internal fuse mechanism.
